zotero.abstractNote: Large Language Models (LLMs) are increasingly deployed behind black-box APIs. While these models are proprietary, they are often vulnerable to model extraction and prompt leakage attacks. In this work, we propose TRAP (Targeted Random Adversarial Prompt), a novel honeypot-based defense mechanism to identify black-box LLM providers. TRAP injects unique, non-functional, and adversarial-looking prompts into the model's context. If these prompts are leaked or used to generate responses, they act as a digital watermark, allowing the model owner to identify the specific black-box API or user that leaked the prompt. We demonstrate the effectiveness of TRAP in identifying black-box models and discuss its potential as a deterrent against unauthorized model usage.
zotero.proceedingsTitle: Findings of the Association for Computational Linguistics: ACL 2024
